name: Lupe Castillo
bio: Lupe Castillo is a community activist and perennial Republican candidate in the heavily Democratic IL-04 congressional district. A 40-year resident of Little Village (South Lawndale), she was born in San Antonio, TX, where Spanish is her first language. She holds an associate degree from Washburne Community Trade School. She ran against Chuy Garcia in 2022 (28.1%) and 2024 (27.3%), and is again the sole Republican candidate in the 2026 IL-04 primary. She is active in the 10th CPD District Advisory Council, the Little Village Community Council, and the Southwest Side GOP. She aligns with the Trump MAGA platform and focuses on immigration enforcement, public safety, and economic issues. FEC committee C00883066 (LUPE CASTILLO FOR CONGRESS) was registered July 10, 2024 but has no 2025–2026 filings on record per FEC.
prior_office: Candidate for IL-04 (2022, 2024); Candidate for IL House District 23 (withdrew 2024).

red_flags.label: Running in D+17 district — longshot candidate
red_flags.detail: IL-04 is among the most Democratic districts in Illinois. Castillo received 28.1% in 2022 and 27.3% in 2024 against Chuy Garcia. Serves as the token Republican opposition in an unwinnable district.

career_history.highlight: 40 years in Little Village; born in San Antonio TX; Spanish is first language; community-based civic engagement.
Ran against Chuy Garcia in 2022 (28.1%) and 2024 (27.3%). Uncontested Republican primary 2026.

policy_platform.position: Border security and immigration reform; finish the border wall; no sanctuary cities; overhaul the asylum process.
Support law enforcement; oppose defunding. Strong crime reduction focus.
No more taxes on Social Security, tips, or overtime. Cut government spending. Energy independence.
Strong support for Israel. NATO burden-sharing for Ukraine.

social_pulse.summary: Limited social media presence. Minimal coverage in major Chicago outlets. Low-profile campaign consistent with prior cycles.
